behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: Real failures usually occur at the boundary between parser (range `A1:A10`), reference resolver, and evaluator. A unit test of `SUM` alone can pass while range expansion or cell binding is wrong (MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE). No external infra beyond in-process workbook state is required.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 to known numeric values, set B1 to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run evaluation, and assert B1 equals the expected total.

behaviors.description: A formula that references a cell containing another formula computes the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: This depends on multiple modules together: dependency resolution, formula evaluation, and value propagation. Pure function tests of arithmetic or parser pieces can miss broken cascade wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=3, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`; evaluate workbook and assert B1=6 and C1=7.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency is detected and reported as a circular reference error.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection correctness is in graph construction plus evaluator traversal. Unit-testing only the cycle detector can miss reference graph build bugs from formula parsing.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=`=B1+1` and B1=`=A1+1`, evaluate, and assert a circular-reference error is returned quickly (no hang/loop).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value and not-found returns an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is function implementation plus table-range resolution and type/key comparison behavior. Mocking the table resolver can hide real lookup-range bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range, evaluate one formula with an existing key and one with a missing key, assert returned value for hit and expected error for miss.

behaviors.description: Nested IF conditions evaluate to the correct branch.
behaviors.justification: Nested IF requires parser nesting + boolean expression evaluation + branch selection. Unit tests of IF alone may not catch parse-tree or precedence errors.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to representative values (e.g., 5, 15, 25), formula `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`, evaluate each case, assert low/medium/high outputs.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on function semantics plus cell-value materialization (empty vs blank-string/null). Isolated unit tests can miss engine-level empty-cell normalization issues.
behaviors.test_description: Set cells with text and empties, evaluate a CONCATENATE formula with separators, and assert empties are skipped (no extra separators/blank segments).

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: This crosses sheet registry, name resolution, and evaluator modules. Mocking sheet lookup can hide real namespace/reference-binding failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1=41, set Sheet1!B1=`=Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate, assert Sheet1!B1=42.

behaviors.description: Changing a referenced cell triggers recalculation of dependents in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily dependency-graph invalidation and scheduler ordering across modules. Unit tests of individual nodes miss ordering/recompute propagation defects.
behaviors.test_description: Build dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1->B1,C1->D1), evaluate once, change A1, recompute, assert all downstream values are updated correctly and consistent with topological dependencies.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and prior dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: Undo correctness spans edit history, formula storage, dependency invalidation, and recomputation. Mocking undo stack or evaluator separately can hide state-coordination bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create dependent formulas, evaluate baseline, edit an upstream formula to change downstream results, invoke undo, assert original formula text is restored and all affected computed values return to baseline.

behaviors.description: Save to disk and load back preserves formulas, values, and floating-point precision.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is serializer/deserializer plus actual filesystem I/O in temp storage. In-memory mocks can hide binary encoding/decoding and file handling issues. No subprocess/network/LLM is required.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and sensitive float values, save to a temp file in the real filesystem, reload into a new workbook instance, assert formula text equality and numeric equality within strict tolerance/no unintended precision drift.
